free_text: Cross sections for alpha particle produced
Silver (8.1 mu-m, 8.505 mg/cm2) and titanium as
one hour with an alpha particle beam of 40 MeV
primary energy at 150 nA. The number of foils in the
stacks was determined as to cover the whole energy
range from the maximal energy down to the threshold.
The thickness and uniformity of the used foils were
determined by weighing the original metal sheets as
well as the individual target pieces. The Ti foils
were interleaved with the Ag foils of the stacks, in
order to monitor the intensity and the energy of the
charged particles by simultaneous re-measurement of
the excitation functions of the monitor reaction over
The energy degradation of the alpha particles through
the stack was estimated by calculation using the
stopping power data and polynomial approximation of
Ziegler. Adaptations in both, beam current and energy
scale, were made based on analysis of the cross section
data for the natTi(a,x)51Cr monitor reaction measured
The uncertainty of the beam energy increases
along the stack due to cumulative straggling effects
and the uncertainty on the determination of the foil
The independent relative errors of the
quadratically and the square root was taken.
